cards.front: What is the P complexity class?
cards.back: The set of decision problems that can be solved by a deterministic Turing machine in polynomial time

cards.front: What is the NP complexity class?
cards.back: The set of decision problems that can be verified in polynomial time, but may not be solvable in polynomial time
A decision problem that is at least as hard as the hardest problems in P, and is not known to be in P
A decision problem that is at least as hard as every problem in NP, and every problem in NP can be reduced to it in polynomial time
A tree-like data structure that is used to make decisions based on conditions and rules
